Thought 3: The murder weapon was a vial of acid. Irving has a clear connection to such substances through his script research and documented purchases. He also had a vial in his possession and was seen carrying it. His motive—Uma threatening to expose his plagiarism—is directly personal and could have provoked a violent response. Addison, while present at the gym and having a motive related to theft (which Uma may have discovered), has no established link to the murder weapon. His custom-made suit might suggest ill-gotten gains from theft, but it doesn't directly implicate him in the acid murder. Irving's behavior, including staying late (consistent with the crime scene) and possessing the vial, points strongly to him.
